title: Doctor-Patient Association
prompt_md:
## Goal

Model a **unidirectional association** between `Doctor` and `Patient`. A doctor keeps track of which patients they see, but patients do not track their doctors, and neither owns the other.

## Input

- Line 1: integer `D` (1 <= D <= 10), the number of doctors.
- Next `D` lines: a doctor name (single word).
- Next line: integer `P` (1 <= P <= 10), the number of patients.
- Next `P` lines: a patient name (single word).
- Next line: integer `A`, the number of associations.
- Next `A` lines: two integers `d p` (0-indexed) meaning doctor `d` sees patient `p`.
- Final line: integer `q`, a doctor index to query.

## Output

Print the queried doctor's name followed by a colon, then each of their patients' names each preceded by a space, in the order they were associated. If the doctor has no patients, print just the name and colon. End with a newline.

Example: a doctor `Alice` seeing `Charlie` then `Eve` prints `Alice: Charlie Eve`.

## Notes

- A `Doctor` stores **non-owning** pointers to the `Patient` objects it sees.
- Patients and doctors are all created in `main`; neither class creates or destroys the other.

test_cases.label: Patient shared by two doctors, query second

title: Bidirectional Student-Course Association
prompt_md:
## Goal

Model a **bidirectional association** between `Student` and `Course`. A student can enroll in multiple courses, and a course tracks which students are enrolled. Neither object owns the other.

## Input

- Line 1: integer `S` (1 <= S <= 10), number of students.
- Next `S` lines: a student name (single word).
- Next line: integer `C` (1 <= C <= 10), number of courses.
- Next `C` lines: a course name (single word).
- Next line: integer `E`, number of enrollments.
- Next `E` lines: two integers `s c` (0-indexed) meaning student `s` enrolls in course `c`.
- Final line: integer `q`, a course index to query.

## Output

Print the course name followed by `: `, then the number of enrolled students, then each enrolled student's name preceded by a space, in enrollment order. If the course has no students, print the name, `: `, then `0`. End with a newline.

Example: `Math: 2 Alice Bob`.

## Notes

- Each enrollment must update **both** sides of the relationship.
- Implement the linking as a free function `enroll(Student&, Course&)`; you will need a forward declaration so the two classes can refer to each other.

test_cases.label: Enrollment order differs from student order
